title: Minimum Removals to Form a Sorted Subsequence
description: Given a string s consisting of lowercase English letters, your task is to determine the minimum number of characters that must be removed so that the remaining characters form a non-decreasing (lexicographically sorted) subsequence.
A subsequence is obtained by deleting zero or more characters without changing the order of the remaining characters.
Your goal is to keep the longest possible non-decreasing subsequence and remove all other characters.
constraints: s contains only lowercase English letters ('a' to 'z')
Time complexity target: O(n log n)

examples.explanation: The longest non-decreasing subsequence is 'aan' (length 3). The original string has length 6, so the minimum removals are 6 - 3 = 3.
Already sorted. Longest subsequence is 'abc'. Minimum removals = 0.
The longest non-decreasing subsequence is any single character (e.g., 'c', 'b', or 'a'). Minimum removals = 3 - 1 = 2.

edge_cases: Strictly decreasing strings should return s.length - 1.
Very long strings must be handled efficiently (O(n log n)).
notes: This problem is a variant of the Longest Non-Decreasing Subsequence (LNDS).
The optimal solution uses binary search to maintain a patience-sorting style structure.
The minimal removals equal: s.length - LNDS_length.
